summary.brief: Manuel Moreno v. Carol Bosholm. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Moreno reported to the medical facility at Scotland Correctional Institute (SCI), a state prison where he was incarcerated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He complained that 'he had been sick for three to five days,' with symptoms that included a 'sore throat, body aches, sinus pain and pressure, and coughing up green sputum.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Bosholm was the physician on duty at the time. She did not personally examine Moreno, but after reviewing Nurse Sullivan’s notes, she assessed that Moreno had sinus congestion and acute pharyngitis (sore throat), and prescribed amoxicillin.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Several inmates tested positive for the flu (Moreno was not tested for it).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: By Monday afternoon, Moreno’s condition had sharply deteriorated. He complained of a headache and vomiting.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Shortly after he arrived at the hospital, Moreno suffered a generalized grand mal seizure that led to him being in a coma for over a month.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Moreno was tested and confirmed positive for influenza H1N1 (swine flu) and also diagnosed with 'severe sepsis' and MSSA pneumonia ('a bacterial superinfection').
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgment of the district court.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Sore throat, body aches, sinus pain and pressure, coughing up green sputum
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reported being sick for three to five days with the above symptoms

reveal.decision_impact: Would have guided appropriate antiviral treatment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to prison medical facility with symptoms

key_events.event: Patient examined by nurse, symptoms recorded

timeline_phases.name: Initial Assessment and Quarantine
timeline_phases.description: Patient assessed and quarantined with other inmates

key_events.event: Patient quarantined with other symptomatic inmates

timeline_phases.name: Condition Deterioration
timeline_phases.description: Patient's condition worsens over the weekend

key_events.event: Patient's condition deteriorates, no specific monitoring recorded

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Transfer
timeline_phases.description: Patient transferred to hospital after deterioration

key_events.event: Patient transferred to hospital after examination by Dr. Bosholm

timeline_phases.name: Hospitalization
timeline_phases.description: Patient was hospitalized and diagnosed with influenza H1N1 and severe pneumonia

key_events.event: Patient suffered a generalized grand mal seizure

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in prison medical facility with flu-like symptoms
decision_points.prompt: What monitoring instructions should be given for the weekend?

options.description: Instruct staff to monitor oxygen saturation and respiration rates
General instructions to monitor without specific parameters
options.clinical_reasoning: Lack of specific monitoring could miss deterioration

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Concern for infectious disease but no specific monitoring ordered

expected_action_court.description: Specific monitoring of oxygen saturation and respiration rates should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for specific monitoring

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to provide specific monitoring instructions led to delayed detection of deterioration
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Specific monitoring could have prompted earlier intervention
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient diagnosed with severe pneumonia and influenza H1N1

key_findings: Dr. Bosholm's actions did not constitute gross negligence

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Testing could have confirmed diagnosis earlier

factual_timeline.event: Patient reported to prison medical facility with symptoms
Patient transferred to hospital after deterioration
Patient reported symptoms to medical facility
Patient complained of headache and vomiting
Patient suffered a generalized grand mal seizure
Patient reports symptoms to medical facility
Patient's condition deteriorates with headache and vomiting
Patient suffers seizure and is hospitalized

diagnoses.diagnosis: Influenza H1N1, severe sepsis, MSSA pneumonia

treatments.outcome: Patient survived but with severe complications

complications.complication: Generalized grand mal seizure, coma, multiple organ damage

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide adequate monitoring instructions
Failure to provide specific monitoring instructions

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care established
Lack of competent testimony establishing the standard of care or its breach
court_findings.legal_significance: District court's judgment in favor of Dr. Bosholm affirmed

court_findings.finding: No gross negligence or deliberate indifference
